name: Equifax Commercial Credit & Workforce Analytics API
what_it_does: Equifax REST APIs for commercial credit, consumer credit, income/employment verification, and workforce analytics. Enables AI agents to manage consumer credit report and score retrieval for lending and underwriting decisions, handle The Work Number (TWN) employment and income verification for credit underwriting and tenant screening, access commercial credit data including Equifax Commercial Credit for B2B lending decisions, retrieve identity and fraud risk signals for KYC and account opening automation, handle government and social services verification for benefit eligibility, access small business credit data for SMB lending automation, retrieve healthcare payment risk data for patient financial services, manage disability and unemployment claims verification for benefits administration, handle payroll-derived income verification for mortgage and auto lending, and integrate credit and workforce analytics with lending, insurance, HR, and government platforms.
use_cases: Verifying employment and income from mortgage lending agents
Underwriting consumer loans from credit decision agents
Verifying commercial credit from B2B lending agents
Authenticating identity from fraud prevention agents
not_for: Non-permissible purpose credit access without FCRA compliance
Consumer self-service without institutional permissible purpose
best_when: A mortgage lender, auto lender, bank, or insurer using Equifax wants AI agents to automate consumer credit underwriting, income and employment verification via The Work Number, commercial credit risk assessment, identity fraud detection, and government benefit eligibility verification.
avoid_when: FCRA LEGAL RISK: Equifax consumer and workforce data is FCRA-governed — automated adverse decisions (credit denial, employment rejection, housing denial) require FCRA adverse action notice, permissible purpose verification, and dispute rights. The Work Number (TWN) employment verification data requires permissible purpose under FCRA for consumer credit use. 2017 Equifax data breach history means heightened data handling scrutiny; verify current security posture and breach remediation.

auth.notes: Equifax uses OAuth 2.0 and mTLS for high-security credit APIs. Developer portal at developer.equifax.com. Atlanta, Georgia HQ. Public (EFX NYSE). One of the three major US credit bureaus. The Work Number (TWN): 655M+ employment records from 2.8M+ employers. Workforce Solutions (TWN, income verification) is a key differentiator. Competes with TransUnion and Experian for consumer credit; Argyle, Pinwheel for income verification.

pricing.paid_tiers: Per-inquiry credit and TWN verification pricing; annual volume contracts
pricing.notes: Atlanta, Georgia. Founded 1899. Public (EFX NYSE). $5.3B+ annual revenue. One of the three major credit bureaus. The Work Number: 655M+ employment records, 2.8M+ employers. Workforce Solutions division (income/employment verification) is largest revenue segment. 2017 data breach of 147M consumer records — paid $700M+ settlement. Competes with TransUnion and Experian for credit bureau market.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits per OAuth token; product and contract dependent

known_agent_gotchas: FCRA CRIMINAL VIOLATION: Consumer credit and TWN employment data without permissible purpose is a criminal FCRA violation; verify and document permissible purpose for each automated inquiry

TWN employer coverage gaps — The Work Number covers 2.8M+ employers but not all employers participate; gig workers, self-employed, and small employer records may be absent; validate coverage for your customer base
2017 data breach legacy — Equifax 2017 breach created regulatory scrutiny and consent decree obligations; verify current security practices and regulatory compliance status
mTLS certificate management — high-security Equifax APIs require client certificates; PKI infrastructure for certificate rotation and management required
Workforce Solutions vs. Consumer division — The Work Number is in Workforce Solutions; consumer credit is in USIS; separate product contracts and API credentials may be needed

FCRA adverse action timing — FCRA requires adverse action notice within specific timeframes after credit decision; automated decisioning systems must trigger adverse action workflows within compliance deadlines
agent_readiness.security_notes: Credit bureau and workforce analytics. SOC2, ISO27001, FCRA, GLBA, GDPR. OAuth2/mTLS. US/EU. Consumer credit and employment data.

security_score_components.security_notes: Credit bureau. SOC2, FCRA, GLBA, GDPR. OAuth2/mTLS. US. Consumer credit and employment verification data.
